market_context: Markets face severe pressure from Iran war escalation driving oil above $110/barrel and blocking Strait of Hormuz, combined with weak February jobs report showing 92,000 job losses and unemployment rising to 4.4%. Energy stocks outperforming while tech and cyclicals under pressure from stagflation fears.

predictions.reasoning: Dow futures down over 1,000 points pre-market with oil surge and weak jobs data creating perfect storm for equity selloff. SPY tracking S&P 500 down 1.4% pre-market points to sustained selling pressure through session amid stagflation concerns.

Tech-heavy Nasdaq futures down 2.3% pre-market as rising oil prices and bond yields pressure credit-sensitive growth stocks. Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft all down 1.5% premarket indicates broad tech weakness will persist.

Energy sector only positive index pre-market as oil hits $110+ with Iran supply disruptions. Major producers cutting output while Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, creating sustained bullish energy fundamentals despite broader market selloff.

Rising oil prices fueling stagflation fears and pushing Treasury yields higher as Fed rate cut expectations diminish. Flight-to-quality demand offset by inflation concerns and higher rate expectations from energy shock.

Fear gauge already up 24% with geopolitical crisis escalating and weak employment data. Market volatility likely to increase further as investors price in stagflation risks and extended Middle East conflict duration.
